channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Iraola top of Palace shortlist for new manager
item.description: Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola heads Crystal Palace's shortlist of candidates to replace outgoing head coach Oliver Glasner next season.

item.title: How Southampton allegedly spied on Middlesbrough
item.description: Southampton are under investigation for allegedly spying on Championship play-off rivals Middlesbrough. It was not as difficult as one might think.

item.title: Man Utd's Fernandes wins Football Writers' men's award
item.description: Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes wins the Football Writers' Association's men's Footballer of the Year award.

item.title: Shaw named Football Writers' Women's Player of Year
item.description: Manchester City striker Khadija Shaw is crowned the Football Writers' Association Women's Player of the Season.

item.title: Arteta rallies Arsenal for huge Premier League weekend
item.description: As the Premier League title race nears its conclusion, Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta tells his players and the Gunners fans to "stay present and live the moment".

item.title: Jimenez suspended as Bournemouth launch investigation
item.description: AFC Bournemouth drop defender Alex Jimenez from their squad amid an investigation into posts on social media.

item.title: Real Madrid in crisis - inside the conflict and chaos at the Bernabeu
item.description: BBC Sport examines a torrid week for Real Madrid with the iconic club on the brink of a second successive trophyless season.

item.title: Why Carrick may not be shoo-in for Man Utd job
item.description: Manchester United have won the most points in the Premier League since caretaker boss Michael Carrick took over, but the stats still show concerns.

item.title: The million-euro dressing room row - Real Madrid fine star pair
item.description: Real Madrid fine midfielders Aurelien Tchouameni and Federico Valverde following a dressing room row between the pair.

item.title: Maeda fit as O'Neill prepares for last Old Firm derby
item.description: Martin O'Neill is relishing what is "most likely" his final Old Firm match as Celtic chase victory over Rangers to stay in the title hunt.

item.title: England 2006: The Golden Generation - watch trailer
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England’s moment. So why did their World Cup dream end in on-field agony and off-field acrimony?

item.title: Dortmund beat Eintracht Frankfurt in five-goal thriller
item.description: Borussia Dortmund survive a late scare as they hang on to beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2 at Signal Iduna Park to secure second place in the German Bundesliga.

item.title: Arsenal draw at Brighton gives Man City WSL title
item.description: Arsenal play out a 1-1 draw away to Brighton & Hove Albion - a result which gives Manchester City their first WSL title since 2016.

item.title: Crystal Palace fans want honey again - Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner had some unusual answers to questions at his news conference before their Conference League semi-final second leg with Shakhtar Donetsk.

item.title: Arteta pays tribute to Arsenal players & fans after 'incredible night'
item.description: Mikel Arteta says Arsenal "created history together" and praises the atmosphere and energy of the Emirates Stadium crowd, as Arsenal beat Atletico Madrid 2-1 on aggregate to reach their first Champions League final in 20 years.

item.title: Has Rodri absence cost Man City? Or is his importance overstated?
item.description: Rodri's injury coincided with Manchester City's damaging draw at Everton - but there evidence suggests his importance is overplayed.

item.title: Why Spurs fans face Arsenal dilemma
item.description: Why Sunday's Premier League game between West Ham and Arsenal could be viewed as a nightmare for Tottenham - whatever the result.

item.title: Win the first leg and keep clean sheets - how to stand a chance in the play-offs
item.description: Keep a clean sheet and win the first leg. BBC Sport takes a closer look at what it might take to get through the play-offs.

item.title: Alexander-Arnold omission 'mind-boggling' to Rooney
item.description: Former England captain Wayne Rooney says it is "mind-boggling" that Trent Alexander-Arnold was left out of Thomas Tuchel's final squad before the World Cup.

item.title: Motherwell peril awaits Hearts & Rangers' panic for second
item.description: Hearts could win a first top-flight title since 1960 on Wednesday - but there is plenty of football to be played before then. Here is what to expect in the Scottish Premiership.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v boxing world champion Fabio Wardley
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on world champion boxer Fabio Wardley plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's fixtures.

item.title: Bonuses, players on the beach & the phoney war - Pulis' end-of-season insights
item.description: Tony Pulis on why motivating players who have nothing to play for is just one of the challenges facing a manager at the end of the season.

item.title: Maldon & Tiptree owners in court on rape charges
item.description: Maldon & Tiptree owners Barrie Drewitt-Barlow and his husband Scott are remanded in custody.

item.title: FPL talking point: Time to triple captain Haaland?
item.description: If you still have your triple captain chip left in Fantasy Premier League then Manchester City - with two home games this week - could be your inspiration.

item.title: Rohl sure of Rangers stay to lead trophy push
item.description: Danny Rohl is fully "convinced" he will remain in charge of Rangers next season amid pressure and speculation linking him with a move.

item.title: Newbies Boreham Wood & old-timers Rochdale seek EFL place
item.description: Boreham Wood and Rochdale prepare to face off in the National League promotion final at Wembley on Sunday for a place in the EFL.

item.title: Remembering 2012 - why Sunderland away means more to Man Utd than normal
item.description: Remembering how Sunderland fans laughed at visitors Manchester United in 2012 when Sergio Aguero's goal snatched the Premier League title away from them.

item.title: Watch: Kane gives Dunfermline play-off advantage
item.description: Watch Chris Kane's goal in Dunfermline Athletic's 1-0 Scottish Premiership play-off quarter-final first-leg win against Arbroath.

item.title: Why Premier League title is Arsenal's to lose after Man City slip
item.description: Mark Chapman, Charlie Adam, Shay Given and Rory Smith discuss Manchester City’s 3-3 draw at Everton, focusing on how dropped points could impact their Premier League title challenge.

item.title: Man City's title hopes dented despite late Doku stunner in six-goal thriller
item.description: Jeremy Doku's 97th-minute equaliser salvages a draw for Manchester City in a six-goal thriller at Everton.

item.title: Nottingham Forest boost survival hopes with win over Chelsea
item.description: Nottingham Forest increase their hopes of Premier League survival after a 3-1 win over Chelsea at Stamford Bridge, as goals from Taiwo Awoniyi and Igor Jesus help the visitors open a six-point gap over 18th-placed West Ham in the relegation zone.

item.title: Doku scored 'really top goals' in 'really good game' - Guardiola
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says Jeremy Doku scored two "really, really top goals" in their six-goal thriller against Everton, as they lose ground on Arsenal in the Premier League title race.

item.title: All your FPL stats and Premier League team news in one place
item.description: Every key bit of Premier League team news and the most important Fantasy Premier League statistics - all in one place.

item.title: Vibrant and victorious - Villa ready to end 30-year trophy drought
item.description: When Unai Emery arrived at Aston Villa he declared he wanted to win trophies. He is now just a game away from silverware after a crushing Europa League semi-final win against Nottingham Forest.

item.title: Arbroath would retain part-time players in Premiership - Gold
item.description: Arbroath would continue to field part-time players if they were to gain promotion to the Scottish Premiership, believes co-manager David Gold.

item.title: English clubs reach all three European finals for first time
item.description: English teams have reached the finals of three different European competitions in the same season for the first time.

item.title: Cardiff sign keeper Trott permanently after loan
item.description: Cardiff City sign Nathan Trott permanently on a three-year deal following the goalkeeper's promotion-winning season on loan from Copenhagen.

item.title: Man City monitor Vinicius Jr - Friday's gossip
item.description: Manchester City are keen on Real Madrid winger Vinicius Jr, Bayern Munich make contact with Newcastle United over Anthony Gordon, France forward Bradley Barcola could leave PSG in the summer, plus more.

item.title: Glasner's long Palace goodbye sets up 'perfect' finale
item.description: Crystal Palace desperately did not want to be in the Conference League - now they are one game from winning it and creating more club history and a "perfect" finale for boss Oliver Glasner.

item.title: Cherki your man for double gameweek 36 - FPL team of the week
item.description: This week is the final double gameweek of the FPL season, with Manchester City and Crystal Palace both playing twice, so tripling up on Pep Guardiola's side is essential.

item.title: Context key to player moves - NI boss McArdle
item.description: Northern Ireland boss Michael McArdle says "individual context" is key when weighing up whether players are better served playing regularly domestically or moving to a higher level elsewhere and not getting consistent game time.

item.title: 'I wouldn't pay it' - Trump on USA ticket price
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he "wouldn't pay it either" when asked about the potential ticket price to watch his country play Paraguay in their 2026 World Cup opener.

item.title: Kilmarnock's Stryjek 'never had doubt' about return from heart surgery
item.description: Kilmarnock goalkeeper Max Stryjek reveals how he has been living with a ticking time bomb of heart trouble for a decade but never had any doubts he would recover from last year's surgery.

item.title: Beard inquest delayed over Burnley 'bullying' concerns
item.description: The inquest into the death of former Women's Super League football manager Matt Beard is delayed over concerns raised by his family about alleged "bullying".

item.title: How Villa could get sixth into the Champions League
item.description: A host of Premier League clubs will be backing Aston Villa to win the Europa League - as it could secure Champions League qualification for whoever finishes sixth.

item.title: Unmasking the coach who led Man City to WSL title glory
item.description: Who is the Swedish mastermind who has guided Manchester City to a first Women's Super League title for a decade?

item.title: How to follow the Premier League on the BBC this weekend
item.description: BBC Sport Ask Me Anything explains how to follow the Premier League on the BBC this weekend.

item.title: Why resolute Arsenal will believe prolific PSG can be beaten in final
item.description: With Paris St-Germain waiting in the Champions League final, will Arsenal withstand the firepower of one of Europe's most formidable attacks and lift the trophy for the first time on 30 May?

item.title: How did Sheffield Wednesday avoid 15-point deduction?
item.description: Sheffield Wednesday fans thought their team would start next season on -15 points. Now they know they will start on zero - and here's why.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Watch: Late penalty drama as Dunfermline edge out Arbroath
item.description: Aston Oxborough saves the late penalty he conceded as Dunfermline Athletic get past Arbroath to set up a Premiership play-off semi-final against Partick Thistle.

item.title: What is Scotland's most iconic goal? This time - Narey v McLean
item.description: Vote to find out what goal will be crowned Scotland's greatest.

item.title: Arfield not throwing name in for Livingston job
item.description: Interim player-manager Scott Arfield does not intend to apply for the Livingston job and says getting grey hair may put him off the role altogether.

item.title: Nobody can question Hearts' mentality - McInnes
item.description: Derek McInnes believes anyone still questioning whether his Heart of Midlothian side have the courage to handle the heat of the Scottish Premiership title race has not been paying attention.

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: Chelsea favourites to sign Shaw as Man City exit nears
item.description: Chelsea are favourites to sign striker Khadija Shaw on a free contract as an exit from Manchester City nears this summer.

item.title: Buckley named Bristol City Women CEO
item.description: Bristol City Women appoint Hannah Buckley as their new chief executive officer.

item.title: Villa's Staniforth 'at ease' with decision to retire
item.description: Aston Villa midfielder and former England international Lucy Staniforth did not hesitate when she made the decision to retire from football at the end of the season.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
